title: Emergency Medical Treatment and Active Labor Act
summary: Required Medicare-participating hospitals with emergency departments to provide screening and stabilizing treatment for emergency medical conditions, including active labor, regardless of ability to pay.
outcome_summary: Strengthened the right to emergency care and reduced the formal ability of hospitals to refuse urgent patients, which has been especially consequential for low-income Black patients and pregnant Black women navigating unequal healthcare systems.
impact_notes: Not designed specifically for Black Americans, but important because emergency-room access and labor-and-delivery protections matter most where discriminatory access barriers and underinsurance are concentrated.

scores.notes: A durable floor of emergency-care access with particular importance for racialized access disparities.

sources.notes: CMS overview of EMTALA and the obligations it imposes on Medicare-participating hospitals.

title: Healthy Start Initiative
summary: Created a federal maternal and infant health initiative focused on communities with exceptionally high infant mortality and poor maternal health outcomes.
outcome_summary: Targeted maternal and infant health resources toward high-risk communities, which has mattered disproportionately for Black mothers and infants because of persistent racial gaps in mortality, prenatal care, and postpartum support.
impact_notes: Important for addressing racialized maternal and infant health disparities through place-based community investment rather than only clinical reimbursement.

scores.notes: A targeted federal maternal-health program aimed at communities where Black maternal and infant mortality burdens have been especially high.

sources.notes: Official HRSA overview describing Healthy Start's maternal and infant health mission and community targeting.

title: Children's Health Insurance Program Reauthorization Act of 2009
summary: Reauthorized and expanded CHIP, increasing federal support for child coverage, outreach, enrollment, and optional coverage of low-income pregnant women.
outcome_summary: Expanded public health-insurance coverage for children and some pregnant women, which helped narrow coverage gaps affecting Black families and improved access to preventive, dental, and maternal-related care.
impact_notes: Meaningful because Black children are more likely to benefit from public insurance expansions when market-based coverage is unequal or unstable.

scores.notes: A strong child-health and family-coverage expansion with racial-equity significance.

sources.source_title: H.R.2 - Children's Health Insurance Program Reauthorization Act of 2009
sources.notes: Official bill page showing enactment of CHIP reauthorization.

sources.source_title: H.R.2 - Children's Health Insurance Program Reauthorization Act of 2009 Summary
sources.notes: CRS summary describing CHIP reauthorization, outreach, pregnant women coverage, and child health improvements.

title: Maternal, Infant, and Early Childhood Home Visiting Program
summary: Established a federal evidence-based home visiting program for pregnant women and families with young children in communities facing greater barriers and health risks.
outcome_summary: Expanded maternal and early-childhood support services that can improve pregnancy outcomes, family stability, and child development in higher-risk communities, including communities facing entrenched racial disparities in maternal and infant health.
impact_notes: An important maternal-health and early-childhood intervention because it addresses support before and after birth, not only acute care financing.

scores.notes: Evidence-based maternal and early-childhood support with strong relevance to racial disparities in maternal and infant outcomes.

sources.source_title: Maternal, Infant, and Early Childhood Home Visiting (MIECHV) Program
sources.notes: Official HRSA overview of the MIECHV program, its target populations, and its intended outcomes.
Demonstrates the continuing national scale and reach of the home-visiting program.
